description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly white surface, featuring subtle gray and brown discolorations. The stone has a roughly oval form with a slightly flattened side and visible linear grooves or indentations on one face, suggesting either natural striations or minor tool marks. Its surface texture appears rough and unpolished, with some areas showing minor pitting and weathering. The coloration is mostly pale, with some faint mottling and patches of darker hues, likely from exposure to the elements. The stone's mass and density suggest it is composed primarily of a hard, durable rock such as marble or limestone. It would be commonly found as a natural obstacle or decorative element in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped public spaces.
description: An irregular, white stone with rough texture, faint grooves, and subtle gray-brown mottling. Its surface is weathered and unpolished, giving it a natural, rugged appearance.
description_view_0: This view shows the stone's broadest face with three vertical grooves and some brownish discoloration.
description_view_1: This angle highlights a smoother, less marked side with faint linear indentations and a mostly white surface.
description_view_2: Here, the stone's rounded back is visible, showing more subtle mottling and a less defined surface texture.
description_view_3: This view presents the stone's most convex side, with a darker, more weathered appearance and minimal visible markings.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular linear grooves and faint mottling, with no regular or artificial patterning.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished with minor pitting and discoloration, indicating moderate natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts gently with paved surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements.
